title: Check LED polarity with a multimeter
description: Use a digital multimeter's diode mode to identify a loose 5 mm LED's anode and cathode before wiring. Disconnect power, work on a dry surface, and wear safety goggles.

dialogue.text:
Set the LED and multimeter on a dry, non-conductive surface. Put on safety goggles and keep all power sources disconnected.
We'll use diode-test mode to find the positive lead.

Switch the multimeter to diode mode and confirm the probes are in the diode sockets. Keep fingers behind the guards.
Touch the red probe to the LED's long leg (anode) and the black probe to the short leg (cathode) without letting probes or leads touch.
The LED should glow faintly or read about 1.8 V. Reverse the probes; it should stay dark or display OL. Stop if the LED heats up or sparks.

Great! The glowing orientation marks the anode.
Turn off the meter, disconnect the probes, and store the LED for your next circuit.
